title: Multi-speaker Attention Alignment for Multimodal Social Interaction

problem.description: Current multimodal large language models (MLLMs) struggle to effectively exploit multimodal cues in complex multi-person social settings
problem.specific_issue: Adding visual information doesn't consistently improve, and can even degrade performance in multi-person settings
problem.root_cause: Cross-modal alignment in multi-person videos is significantly weaker and less focused compared to general object-centric datasets

components: Dynamic cross-modal head selection strategy to identify attention heads responsible for visual-text grounding
Adaptive social-aware attention bias to amplify attention scores between visual and textual tokens belonging to the same speaker

evaluation.results: Approach strengthens the model's focus on areas relevant to the active speaker
